untranslatable_reason: Box spread arbitrage requires multi-leg options pricing, strike spreads, net debit/credit calculations, and expiration value comparisons. These are derivatives portfolio mechanics that cannot be expressed using the available price-action and technical indicators (RSI, MACD, moving averages, etc.). The signal requires: (1) real-time options chain data with implied volatility, (2) ability to calculate spread net costs across multiple strikes and expirations, (3) comparison of position cost to intrinsic box value, and (4) tracking of multi-leg positions. None of these can be computed from OHLCV data alone.
translation_notes: This is a fundamental options arbitrage strategy that relies on options pricing theory and multi-leg position management, not technical price action. A backtest engine using only price-based indicators cannot implement this rule. Would require: options pricing module, options chain data source, and portfolio-level position tracking across multiple legs and strikes.
